title: Rich Crispy Egyptian Grape Tagine
description: A fatty and crispy Egyptian dish featuring grapes, perfect for a date night, made by boiling and then frying the ingredients to achieve a crispy texture.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the grapes, coriander, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix together the flour and a pinch of salt.
Remove the grape mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Dip each grape into the flour mixture, coating it evenly.
Heat about 1/2 inch of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry the coated grapes until they are crispy and golden brown.
Drain the fried grapes on paper towels and serve hot.
